prompt:
We're porting the release tooling off bash, and the one script nobody trusts is the runner shim — the old bash version word-split a deploy message with spaces in it into three arguments once, and since then every wrapper change gets audited by hand. PowerShell arrays don't word-split, so this time I want the shim to PROVE what it forwarded, every run.

Build `relay.ps1` in the repo root. Invocation: `relay.ps1 <report> <child.ps1> [args...]`. It runs `<child.ps1>` as a child pwsh process (non-interactively, no profile), forwarding every remaining argument EXACTLY as received — spaces preserved, empty strings preserved (yes, we pass those on purpose), values starting with `-` or `--` passed through without being eaten as parameters, glob-looking things like `notes/*.md` left alone, argument order untouched. The child's stdout and stderr flow through untouched, and the relay exits with the child's exit code.

The proof is the report file, written to `<report>` (relative paths resolve against the invoker's working directory). Format, LF line endings: first `argc=<n>` for the count of forwarded arguments, then one line per argument shaped `[<index>] <length>:<value>`, then after the child finishes a final `exit=<code>` line. So a run forwarding `hotfix for cart totals`, an empty string, and `-Verbose` records:

argc=3
[0] 22:hotfix for cart totals
[1] 0:
[2] 9:-Verbose
exit=0

Edge handling: fewer than two arguments → single stderr line `relay: usage: relay.ps1 <report> <child.ps1> [args...]`, exit 64, and no report file. Child path that isn't a file → `relay: no such script: <path>` on stderr, exit 65, and again the report must NOT be created (a half-written report is worse than none — the auditor diffs these).

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_relay.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P1 item 5: bash-argfwd's DEBUG lesson (word-splitting wrapper) recast as a BUILD spec — the pin is that pwsh arrays do NOT word-split and @args forwards faithfully. Probed 2026-07-16 through three pwsh layers (harness -> relay -> child): empty strings, consecutive empties, '  pad  ', '-', '--target=web1', 'notes/*.md', '0' all arrive byte-exact, and $LASTEXITCODE propagates (exit 9 case). Teeth against plausible wrong builds: string-joining then splitting collapses 'a b  c' and drops empties; Start-Process -ArgumentList mangles empties; truthiness filtering drops ''; adding [CmdletBinding()] to the relay makes it eat '-Verbose' as a common parameter (that is why the tests forward '-Verbose' literally). The child fixtures are harness-generated in _t so files/ ships only the test. Differentiation from bash-argfwd: build vs debug kind, and the pinned artifact is a length-prefixed round-trip manifest plus exit-code propagation, not a spool format fix.
